w:r.w:t: n inbound vessel is entered and stored into inventory once received
dit Voyage No.; What is used to searc
Voyage No.; Where the Voyage # is entered prior to creation/where it will show when searched
upon vessel discharge determines when cargo can be shipped out via CT
SST/Cust Mark (Our internal mark for recognition, ex. NLS1
was manifested on the PL and

w:r.w:t: Where BOLs are created for the customer/our records
Enter Ship to info from release (ex. below)
Go voyage no. section and pick the relevant vessel in which the signified release states
Go to inbound BOL section and pick the relevant product stated in the release
Edit Booking No.; The IP order number is entered (ex. 10230268)
After this is inputted, the information from the rail car/size/etc. should populate automatically
st is provided by the driver, you can either input this info manually or wait till the end of the day and this info will populate
Scan Paper in; Scanning paper received from rail cars/vans into inventory
(usually do this manually in the office via Paper Inbound section)
Scan roll into the Roll Num Section

w:r.w:t: relevant info and hit the “Associate All” button
; The tab used when shipping paper out on the designated vessel
Once scanned out (Utilize scan website, steps in another section
customer inventory rolls should move over the outbound items section
After all rolls are scanned out and sent, the rolls in inventory tab should read “0”

w:r.w:t: When paper is sent out on a
in via scanner email (reference later steps)
Enter BOL number for the specific POD (found on sheet/CT)
Sometimes the software lags when connection is lost, wait for reconnect rather than reloading the page

w:r.w:t: here inventory along with each shipment sent out on a specific BL can be located
are shown in no specific order, so this step can be disregarded)
What the product is (all are shown in no specific order, so this step can be disregarded)
Vessel, Discharge date, Product, Customer/SST Mark, how many were discharged/how many are left is shown

w:r.w:t: Go to MFO Outbound section in alternate switchboard
Shipment Plan ID; Same as previously input inbound
Vehicle ID; Same as previously input inbound
MFO NO.; Same as previously input inbound
NO. of Units; Same as previously input inbound
SSP; Same as previously input inbound

w:r.w:t: Units to Ship; Same as previously input outbound
Release number; Use SSP from previously input outbound
Use BOL number from the CT outbound you create and give to the driver
Use the mark we use for the lumber (ex. NLS1)
